behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is a local deterministic parsing/segmentation rule. The failure boundary is within segmentation logic itself, with no required module handoff, filesystem, subprocess, network, or model output. Higher levels were considered and rejected because the behavior text is only about how statements are segmented ("separate segments, not one monolithic block"), not cross-module orchestration or real infra effects.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmentation function with a multi-line shell block containing newline-separated statements and assert it returns distinct segments in order rather than one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: The reported incident came from interaction between segmentation and trigger classification (`not_a_trigger` on the whole block). Minimum real-failure boundary is module handoff (segmenter -> classifier), not a single function. Integration brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess required, no AI/ML dependence, no multi-agent chain. System/Agentic/Workflow were rejected because behavior text focuses on classification of a shell block pattern, not external infra or model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the hook’s command-classification pipeline end-to-end in-process with input like `VAR=...\nOTHER=...\ngh pr create ...` and assert the final classification is PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is the negative case of the same cross-module detection path (segmentation + trigger matcher). A unit matcher test could miss boundary bugs where segmented commands are recombined or misrouted. Integration brake checks all negative for higher levels: no required real subprocess/network, no AI output dependence, no multi-step agent pipeline. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text being purely trigger discrimination between `gh pr create` and `gh issue create`.
behaviors.test_description: Run the in-process classification pipeline with multi-line assignments ending in `gh issue create ...` and assert classification is non-PR trigger (e.g., `not_a_trigger` for PR-create logic).

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans detection output, identifier reconstruction, and persistence formatting/writing. The failure appears at local module boundaries plus filesystem persistence. Integration is sufficient because local filesystem I/O is allowed at this level and no real OS event semantics/subprocess/network are required. System was considered and rejected: behavior text says "reconstruct and persist" an identifier, not real subprocess exit handling or filesystem watcher/event timing.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp workspace, feed a detected PR-create event through the hook path that reconstructs PR identifier and writes state; assert state file exists and contains expected identifier fields.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit cross-component contract validation (PostToolUse writer -> pre-tool enforcement reader). Real failure boundary is schema/state handoff between modules. Integration brake: no mandatory real network/subprocess; no AI/ML output; not a multi-agent chain. System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text is about local state compatibility and enforcement by another hook, which can be tested in-process with shared filesystem fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Run post-tool state writer to produce gate state, then invoke pre-tool enforcement reader/validator against that file and assert enforcement decision is triggered as expected.
